extraction.one_line_summary: LUTSENKO Alexey won from an 8-man early break that gained 6+ minutes, surviving solo after attacks on the final climb while GC riders marked each other.
tactical_patterns: Van Avermaet allowed to lead breakaway 6+ minutes as GC teams lacked motivation to chase non-threat riders.
Alaphilippe attacked final climb for symbolic time gain after yesterday's penalty, showing psychological need for redemption.
Bernal protected by Kwiatkoski while other GC favorites neutralized each other, none willing to test form early.

rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: drove the entire breakaway effort then survived solo attacks on final climb, exceptional engine for long efforts
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: launched decisive attack on climb in first Tour, finishing 4th on mountaintop at 24 shows elite climbing potential
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: CCC's sponsor troubles may have motivated aggressive breakaway tactics for visibility rather than GC ambitions
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: attacked solely for symbolic gesture after time penalty, wasting energy outside tactical necessity
rider_signals.OSS Daniel: neutral: breakaway role suggests Bora preserving Sagan energy, using domestique for intermediate sprint instead
